threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' - this is Dalston, mate, we don’t do subterranean.
Last night on the 133, a guy was passionately explaining why he believes Brixton's buskers should be licensed. Didn’t realise it was such a big debate!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I walked by a toddler sharing a donut with a stray cat by the canal. That mix of chaos and kindness encapsulates Neukölln perfectly.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do they have a 'silent disco' in public parks where everyone dances with headphones on. Feels surreal under the trees!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name all the taco trucks on 16th without Googling. The hidden gems speak more than the tourist spots.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a pigeon taking a ride on the back of a Muni bus today. Only in SF do even the birds know how to travel smart.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is all Gaudí—don't forget the hidden plazas where locals gather, especially after 9pm. That's where the real vibe is.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still trying to find a nearby supermarket without resorting to Google Maps, while locals know the small shops off the beaten path.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still wait for the bus at the stop instead of playing the game of spotting which one will actually show up next.
Heard a group of teens on the 3 bus debating if the Brixton Academy's better at midnight or 3am. Their absolute passion for the scene was wild.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a dude serenading his cat on the windowsill while the bus was delayed for the third time that week. Classic Neukölln vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you grab a Kebab and watch a full-on street art battle unfold right in front of you. It’s a wild mix of food and talent!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know the secret back entrance to Bi-Rite when the line gets crazy. Tourists are still out front waiting for scoops.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in full tuxedo with a bouquet of roses on the 47 bus. Turns out he was late for his own wedding across town. Pure SF magic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think the Gràcia street festivals are all about the parades. It's really about the late-night jam sessions on balconies that you have to experience.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they aggressively search for 'local' Spanish cafés but end up in the tourist traps on Carrer Gran.
